sprint: Sprint 37 -- V0.27.11 Bug Fixes (I-036 polkit poweroff + I-037 US-308 canary regression from Drain 22)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #10 in the V0.27 chain. Drain 22 (2026-05-14 night) surfaced double P0 chain-blocker: I-036 systemctl poweroff PolicyKit auth fail (latent since V0.24.1 -- all drains 10-22 likely hard-crashed) + I-037 prior_boot_clean canary false-positive that masked I-036 for 11 days. RCA findings: Spool's hypothesis that US-330 retry-fallback returned default 1 is empirically WRONG -- US-330's retry-fallback returns [] which propagates to NULL, not 1. The actual root cause is US-308's (2026-05-09) ladder probe pattern 'PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at' matching an INTENT marker emitted BEFORE the failing subprocess.run call. Branched from sprint/sprint36-bugfixes-V0.27.10 (V0.27.10 tip @ c6e218a) per chain-end-merge rule.
createdBy: Marcus (PM) Session 35 2026-05-15 placeholder shell; Ralph + CIO cooked final contract Session 36 2026-05-15 per CIO directive '/init-ralph + use your plan skills and RCA each one and then fix each one'.

testBaseline.note: Inherited from V0.27.10 / Sprint 36 baseline (commit 6184a7f). Re-snapshot post-V0.27.11 ship.

sprintNotes: RCA-driven contract: Spool's Bug #2 hypothesis (US-330 retry-fallback default = 1) was empirically wrong. Actual root cause is US-308's ladder-probe pattern semantics (matches INTENT marker emitted BEFORE the failing subprocess.run). US-330's retry code is innocent and stays untouched -- the race-guard intent is preserved.

Fix strategy A+G locked with CIO 2026-05-15: Option A (polkit rule grants mcornelison org.freedesktop.login1.power-off) for I-036; Strategy G (success marker emitted after subprocess.run returncode==0 + ERROR-level + raise on non-zero) for I-037, with success marker emitted in shutdown_handler._executeShutdown. Runtime contract test pins handler-emit and probe-pattern together.
US-343 INCLUDED in V0.27.11 per CIO 2026-05-15 (not deferred). Read-only audit script + findings template; populated post-deploy from a host with SSH to chi-eclipse-01.
Spool will verify polkit + canary fixes via bench mock BEFORE Drain 23 real drain (per Spool 2026-05-15 note).
Drain 23 pre-conditions: battery >= 8h on charger + V0.27.11 deployed + Spool bench-mock verification complete + Drive 12 retest still required from V0.27.10 chain bigDoD.

stories.title: I-036 + Strategy-G hardening: polkit rule + _executeShutdown emits success marker, ERROR+raise on failure
stories.intent: Fix the PolicyKit auth fail (Option A: polkit rule at /etc/polkit-1/rules.d/50-eclipse-obd-poweroff.rules grants user mcornelison the org.freedesktop.login1.power-off action). Additionally harden shutdown_handler._executeShutdown: (1) emit SHUTDOWN_SUCCESS_MARKER ('PowerDownOrchestrator: poweroff accepted by systemd (graceful shutdown initiated)') after subprocess.run returncode==0 -- this is the honest canary boot_reason re-points at in US-342; (2) ERROR-log and raise ShutdownHandlerError on non-zero returncode instead of warning-and-swallow (the 11-day I-036 cover-up). Latent since V0.24.1 deploy 2026-05-04 -- every drain 10-22 likely hard-crashed at buck-dropout floor (~3.30V).

tests/pi/hardware/test_shutdown_handler_poweroff_auth.py (NEW -- 3 tests: success path emits marker verbatim; failure path raises ShutdownHandlerError + ERROR-logs with stderr; failure path does NOT emit success marker)
deploy/deploy-pi.sh (UPDATE -- idempotent install step rsyncs polkit rule to /etc/polkit-1/rules.d/ root-owned 0644)
doNotTouch: battery_health_log close-out path (writes runtime_seconds + end_timestamp BEFORE shutdown invocation; preserve order)
V0.24.1 ladder stage WARNING/IMMINENT/TRIGGER logic in src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (orthogonal -- ladder fires correctly, only terminal poweroff fails)
ShutdownHandler legacy 30s timer + 10% low-battery trigger code paths (US-216 suppression flag governs them; not in I-036 scope)

groundingRefs.value: Drain 22 journal evidence: 22:53:09 _executeShutdown 'Shutdown command returned non-zero: 1. stderr: Call to PowerOff failed: Interactive authentication required.'
eclipse-obd.service line 49: 'User=mcornelison' (unprivileged); no AmbientCapabilities, no polkit rule (deploy/polkit-rules/ does not exist pre-fix), no sudoers NOPASSWD for poweroff
shutdown_handler.py line 247 subprocess.run(['systemctl','poweroff']) + lines 254-258 warning-only failure handling (silent-failure anti-pattern)

acceptance: Pre-flight: rg 'CAP_SYS_BOOT|polkit|NOPASSWD' deploy/ src/pi/ shows no pre-existing power-off grant for mcornelison (smoke baseline)

Root cause documented in shutdown_handler.py file-header modification-history block: Option A (polkit) chosen because PolicyKit-native, no orchestrator code change for auth, no privilege escalation beyond power-off, preserves systemd graceful unit-shutdown sequence (so Reached-target-Shutdown also persists as 2nd canary signal)
SHUTDOWN_SUCCESS_MARKER constant exported by src.pi.hardware.shutdown_handler and emitted at WARNING level only after subprocess.run returncode==0
On non-zero returncode or TimeoutExpired: logger.error(...) AND raise ShutdownHandlerError(...) -- never silently warn-and-return (this is the 11-day cover-up pattern we are closing)

Synthetic regression tests in tests/pi/hardware/test_shutdown_handler_poweroff_auth.py: success path emits SHUTDOWN_SUCCESS_MARKER verbatim (caplog); failure path raises with stderr in exception message + ERROR-level log; failure path does NOT emit success marker
Polkit rule file deploy/polkit-rules/50-eclipse-obd-poweroff.rules grants ONLY org.freedesktop.login1.power-off (NOT reboot/halt/suspend) and ONLY for subject.user mcornelison
deploy-pi.sh installs the polkit rule under /etc/polkit-1/rules.d/50-eclipse-obd-poweroff.rules idempotently (rsync overwrites only on content change; PolicyKit auto-reloads; no daemon-reload needed)
deploy-pi.sh dry-run mode prints the install step without executing (matches existing --dry-run pattern)

Real-world drain 23 gate (post-deploy): prior boot journal contains BOTH 'Reached target Shutdown' / 'Powering off' AND 'PowerDownOrchestrator: poweroff accepted by systemd' -- Spool verifies via grep
invariants: battery_health_log close-out still writes runtime_seconds + end_timestamp BEFORE poweroff invocation (analytics integrity)
V0.24.1 ladder WARNING/IMMINENT/TRIGGER state-machine progression preserved (orthogonal)
No new privilege escalation beyond power-off specifically (polkit rule scope = power-off only)
ShutdownHandler legacy 30s timer + 10% low-battery suppression behavior (US-216) unchanged
ShutdownHandlerError class existed pre-fix; this story raises it from _executeShutdown where pre-fix the method returned normally on warning

feedback.filesActuallyTouched: src/pi/hardware/shutdown_handler.py (added SHUTDOWN_SUCCESS_MARKER constant + replaced _executeShutdown to emit on success, ERROR+raise on failure); tests/pi/hardware/test_shutdown_handler_poweroff_auth.py (NEW -- 3 tests); tests/pi/hardware/test_shutdown_handler_legacy_suppress.py (calibration: mocks return returncode=0 for the new raise contract); tests/pi/power/test_ladder_vs_legacy_race.py (same calibration); deploy/polkit-rules/50-eclipse-obd-poweroff.rules (NEW); deploy/deploy-pi.sh (added step_install_polkit_poweroff + main-body call)
feedback.grounding: Root cause: eclipse-obd.service runs as User=mcornelison (unprivileged) with no polkit / CAP_SYS_BOOT / sudoers NOPASSWD grant for org.freedesktop.login1.power-off, plus _executeShutdown silently warning-logged non-zero returncode. Option A (polkit rule) chosen because PolicyKit-native, no orchestrator code change for auth, scope-limited to power-off, preserves systemd graceful unit sequence (so Reached-target-Shutdown also persists as 2nd canary signal). Strategy G hardening adds ERROR+raise so any future auth regression pages loudly at TRIGGER instead of silently swallowing.

stories.title: I-037 root cause: repoint LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN at the post-success marker + runtime contract test
stories.intent: Fix the canary lie. RCA (Ralph 2026-05-15): Spool's hypothesis (US-330 retry-fallback default 1) is WRONG -- US-330's retry-fallback returns [] which propagates to priorBootClean=None / NULL, not 1. The actual root cause is US-308's (2026-05-09) ladder probe pattern 'PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at' which matches the orchestrator's INTENT marker emitted by _enterTrigger() at orchestrator.py:887-890 BEFORE the failing subprocess.run call. Drain 22 evidence: the intent marker is in the prior-boot journal even when poweroff FAILED (I-036). Fix: repoint LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN to 'PowerDownOrchestrator: poweroff accepted by systemd' -- the post-success marker emitted by US-341's hardened _executeShutdown ONLY on returncode==0. Add runtime contract test: caplog over a mocked-success _executeShutdown invocation must contain LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN, so future log-line refactor breaks the test at PR time instead of at next drain. US-330's race-guard retry code is INNOCENT of this regression and stays untouched.

filesToTouch: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (UPDATE -- LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN constant repointed at the post-success marker; docstring + file modification history updated; _readBootList retry code UNTOUCHED)

tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason_canary.py (NEW -- 4 tests: drain-22 intent-only journal -> priorClean=False; graceful journal with success marker -> priorClean=True; runtime contract test (caplog over mocked-success _executeShutdown contains LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN); static contract test (LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN is substring of SHUTDOWN_SUCCESS_MARKER))

tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason.py (UPDATE-IF-NEEDED -- US-308's TestProbeLadderGraceful fixture strings may hard-code old pattern; if so, update to use SHUTDOWN_SUCCESS_MARKER + add modification history row)
doNotTouch: _readBootList retry-count + retry-backoff parameters (US-330 race-guard intent is correct AND its retry-fallback returns [] -> NULL, not 1; it is INNOCENT of this regression)
SHUTDOWN_MARKERS tuple in boot_reason.py (systemd tail markers are still the primary signal -- this story only changes the ladder *probe* pattern)
_probeLadderGraceful function body (only the pattern constant it greps for changes; the function logic is correct)
TD-051 orphan-cleanup unit-ordering (separate cleaner-fix candidate; not in V0.27.11 scope)
src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (US-341 owns shutdown_handler; US-342 only touches boot_reason.py)

groundingRefs.value: US-330 commit 76aa773 diff: _readBootList retries 3x on --list-boots failure; if all retries fail returns []; caller's `priorEntry = max(...default=None)` returns None; detectBootReason returns BootReasonReport(priorBootClean=None) -> DB writes NULL via `None if report.priorBootClean is None else int(...)`. CANNOT produce a 1.

orchestrator.py:887-890 _enterTrigger emits 'PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at %.3fV -- initiating poweroff' BEFORE delegating to shutdown_handler. Drain 22 evidence: this line at 22:53:08 is followed at 22:53:09 by the poweroff FAILURE -- intent marker survives a failed shutdown.

boot_reason.py:214 LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN = 'PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at' (US-308 2026-05-09). Combined with _probeLadderGraceful's bool(output.strip()) on a non-empty journalctl -g result -> priorClean promoted to True on every drain that reached TRIGGER stage, regardless of poweroff success.

acceptance: Pre-flight: rg 'LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN|SHUTDOWN_SUCCESS_MARKER|_probeLadderGraceful' src/pi/ tests/pi/ -- map call sites + drift surface

Root cause documented in boot_reason.py file-header modification-history block: pre-fix pattern matched orchestrator INTENT marker (emitted BEFORE subprocess.run); fix repoints at post-success marker (emitted only AFTER subprocess.run returncode==0)
Spool's wrong-hypothesis acknowledged in modification-history: US-330 retry-fallback returns [] -> NULL, not 1. US-330 retry code stays untouched.
LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN value matches the SHUTDOWN_SUCCESS_MARKER substring verbatim (static contract test enforces it)

Synthetic regression PASS-FAIL pair: hard-crash scenario (drain-22-style prior-boot journal with TRIGGER intent marker but NO success marker) -> priorBootClean=False; graceful scenario (prior boot journal with success marker present) -> priorBootClean=True

Runtime contract test: caplog over a mocked-success ShutdownHandler._executeShutdown invocation contains LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN as a substring of an emitted log line (drift-gate: future refactor of either side breaks test at PR time)
US-330 race-guard intent preserved: _readBootList retry count + backoff unchanged; SD-I/O contention false-negative defense intact
Real-world drain 23 gate: canary credibly distinguishes I-036-fixed (prior_boot_clean=1) vs I-036-still-broken (prior_boot_clean=0)
invariants: US-330 race-guard retry count + backoff preserved (no regression on SD-I/O-contention false-negative)
startup_log writer call-site contract preserved (no schema or column rename)
boot_reason.py public API surface preserved (only LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN string value changes + docstring)
_probeLadderGraceful function logic unchanged (only the constant it consumes changes)
Tail-based shutdown-marker scan (SHUTDOWN_MARKERS tuple) preserved as primary signal

feedback.filesActuallyTouched: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (repointed LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN from 'PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at' to 'PowerDownOrchestrator: poweroff accepted by systemd' + modification-history block); tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason_canary.py (NEW -- 4 tests: drain-22 hard-crash returns False; graceful returns True; runtime drift gate; static substring pin)
feedback.grounding: Root cause: US-308's (2026-05-09) ladder probe pattern matched the orchestrator INTENT marker (PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at) emitted by orchestrator.py:887 BEFORE the failing subprocess.run in shutdown_handler.py. Drain 22 evidence confirms intent marker is in the prior-boot journal even when poweroff FAILED. Spool's initial hypothesis (US-330 retry-fallback default=1) is empirically wrong -- the retry-fallback returns [] which propagates to NULL. US-330 retry code is innocent and untouched. Fix: repoint pattern at US-341's new SHUTDOWN_SUCCESS_MARKER (emitted only AFTER returncode==0). Runtime contract test couples handler-emit to probe-pattern so future drift breaks at PR time.

stories.title: Historical drain 10-22 canary re-audit script + findings template
stories.intent: Per Spool 2026-05-15 offer + CIO 2026-05-15 directive (INCLUDE in V0.27.11, do NOT defer): build a read-only audit script that connects to chi-eclipse-01 via SSH, reads startup_log rows for drains 10-22 (V0.24.1 deploy 2026-05-04 onward), greps each prior-boot journal for the new SHUTDOWN_SUCCESS_MARKER vs the INTENT marker, and emits a corrected per-drain verdict (graceful / hard-crash / indeterminate). Writes a Markdown findings document. Script is read-only -- no UPDATE, no DELETE, no INSERT against live tables. Findings populate post-V0.27.11 deploy when chi-eclipse-01 is reachable; the script + a template findings doc ship in V0.27.11 so the audit trail and F-008/F-012 regression_manifest cross-link exist before chain merge.

offices/pm/findings/2026-05-15-drain-10-22-canary-re-audit.md (NEW -- findings template; populated post-deploy from a host with SSH access to chi-eclipse-01; cross-link from F-008 regression_manifest lastValidated audit trail)

Audit script reads startup_log + journalctl --no-pager grep on Pi via SSH (using deploy/addresses.sh PI_HOST / PI_USER conventions)
Per-drain verdict logic: success marker present -> graceful; success marker absent AND intent marker present -> hard-crash; neither -> indeterminate
Script is read-only: no UPDATE, no DELETE, no INSERT verbs against the Pi-side SQLite DB (grep src for INSERT|UPDATE|DELETE -- only SELECT)
--dry-run mode prints SSH commands without executing (so the script can be validated on a Windows dev machine without chi-eclipse-01 reachable)

feedback.filesActuallyTouched: offices/pm/scripts/audit_historical_drain_canary.py (NEW -- 246-line read-only audit script: ssh + sqlite3 + journalctl grep; --dry-run prints SSH commands without executing; --output writes Markdown findings); offices/pm/findings/2026-05-15-drain-10-22-canary-re-audit.md (NEW -- template + interpretation legend + F-008/F-012 cross-refs)
feedback.grounding: Per Spool 2026-05-15 offer + CIO directive to INCLUDE in V0.27.11. Script reads startup_log via SSH+sqlite3-json, then for each row greps the prior-boot journal for SUCCESS_MARKER ('PowerDownOrchestrator: poweroff accepted by systemd') vs INTENT_MARKER ('PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at'). Verdict: success-marker present -> graceful; intent-only -> hard-crash; neither -> indeterminate. Read-only: SELECT only, no UPDATE/INSERT/DELETE; --dry-run smoke validated on Windows dev without chi-eclipse-01 reachable. Findings populate post-deploy.

bigDefinitionOfDone: US-341 IRL: systemctl poweroff invoked by eclipse-obd.service returns 0; prior-boot journal contains BOTH 'Reached target Shutdown'/'Powering off' AND 'PowerDownOrchestrator: poweroff accepted by systemd'
US-342 IRL: hard-crash scenario -> prior_boot_clean=0; graceful poweroff scenario -> prior_boot_clean=1 (canary tells the truth for the first time since 2026-05-12)

Drain 23 (V0.27 chain gate, post-V0.27.11 deploy + Spool bench-mock pre-verification): Pi powers off within ~5s of TRIGGER + startup_log next boot shows prior_boot_clean=1 + journal contains BOTH systemd shutdown-target lines AND the new success marker
Drive 12 retest still required from V0.27.10 chain bigDoD: server drive_summary analytics fields populated + Approach-1 drive_statistics rows produced
